articles.title: USMNT Stuns Paraguay 4-1 in World Cup 2026 Opener at SoFi Stadium
bullets: The US Men's National Team dominated Paraguay 4-1 on June 12 at SoFi Stadium, Los Angeles.
Folarin Balogun scored twice; Christian Pulisic controlled the midfield throughout the match.
The decisive win sent shockwaves through Group C and elevated US title hopes at home World Cup.

articles.title: Trump Announces US Strike Killed Tren de Aragua Gang Leader in Venezuela
bullets: President Trump said a US strike killed Hector Rusthenford Guerrero Flores, Tren de Aragua's top leader.
Trump called Guerrero Flores 'the infamous leader' and announced the operation on Friday.
The strike marks one of the most high-profile US counter-gang operations in South America.

articles.title: Trump's Name Removed from Kennedy Center After Court-Ordered Deadline
bullets: Workers removed Trump's name from the Kennedy Center facade Saturday, hours after a Friday deadline.
A federal court order required all references to Trump to be removed from the building by Friday.
The ruling follows an ongoing legal battle over Trump's renaming of the arts venue.

articles.title: FISA Section 702 Surveillance Renewal Fails in Congress Before Recess
bullets: Efforts to renew FISA Section 702 failed before the House left for a scheduled recess.
The government says more than 60% of the president's daily intelligence briefing relies on this tool.
The expiration creates a critical gap in signals intelligence collection the White House calls urgent.

articles.title: SpaceX IPO Raises $75B in Largest Market Debut Ever; SPCX Surges 19% on Day One
bullets: SpaceX priced its IPO at $135/share on June 11, implying a $1.75 trillion valuation.
SPCX opened at $150 on Nasdaq June 12 and closed at $161.11, up 19% on its first day.
The debut makes SpaceX one of the world's most valuable companies, rivaling Amazon.

articles.title: Iran Closes Strait of Hormuz as US-Iran Strikes Escalate Into Second Day
bullets: Iran declared the Strait of Hormuz closed to all shipping after a second day of US-Iran strikes.
Three tankers carrying Indian crews were struck in four days amid intensified hostilities.
A US-Iran peace deal framework is reportedly close to signing, with key terms including reopening the strait.

articles.title: US-Iran Peace Deal Framework Finalized; Signing Expected in Coming Days
bullets: A purported US-Iran deal framework is finalized, with signing expected within days.
Key terms include complete reopening of the Strait of Hormuz and lifting of US blockade on Iranian ports.
Markets briefly weakened after Trump warned Iran before recovering on progress reports.

articles.title: Pope Leo XIV Blesses Sagrada Familia's New Tower — Now World's Tallest Church
bullets: Pope Leo XIV blessed Barcelona's Sagrada Familia new tower, now the world's tallest church.
The mass marked 100 years since Antoni Gaudí's death, drawing enormous global attention.
The completed tower caps more than a century of construction on the iconic UNESCO site.

articles.title: Northern Ireland Violence Erupts: Anti-Immigrant Riots Rock Belfast for Second Night
bullets: Protesters set fires and hurled bricks at police in Belfast during a second night of violence.
The unrest was sparked by a stabbing incident and quickly escalated into anti-immigrant riots.
Police warned more violence was likely as tensions remained high across Northern Ireland.

articles.title: Somali Referee Denied US Entry, Barred from World Cup 2026 Officiating Role
bullets: Somali referee Omar Artan was denied entry to the United States and removed from the World Cup officials list.
Hundreds of supporters welcomed him back in Mogadishu after his removal became public.
FIFA has not publicly commented; the incident raised questions about visa policy during a US-hosted tournament.

articles.title: PM Modi Visits France to Meet Macron in Nice, Review Strategic Partnership
bullets: PM Modi is in France June 13-18, meeting Macron in Nice on June 14 to review bilateral ties.
India-France relations were elevated to a 'special global strategic partnership' earlier this year.
The visit follows India's record 7.7% GDP growth in FY26, reaffirming its major-economy status.

articles.title: President Murmu Attends IMA Passing Out Parade in Uttarakhand
bullets: President Droupadi Murmu presided over the 158th Regular Course and 141st Technical Graduate Course parade.
The ceremony at the Indian Military Academy in Uttarakhand commissioned new officers into the army.
Murmu congratulated graduates and emphasized the importance of national service and discipline.

articles.title: Afghanistan Cricket Team Tours India for Test and ODIs Through June 20
bullets: Afghanistan's cricket team is touring India from June 6-20, playing one Test and three ODIs.
The series is seen as a major step for Afghanistan cricket's development on the world stage.
India's first ODI against Afghanistan drew large crowds ahead of the World Cup tournament.

articles.title: Rahul Gandhi Launches Student Conference Series Starting June 17 in Kota
bullets: Congress leader Rahul Gandhi will hold large student conferences starting June 17 in Kota.
Follow-up events are planned in Allahabad on July 10, Patna on July 11, and Delhi on July 14.
The initiative targets student voters ahead of upcoming state and national electoral cycles.

articles.title: TCS Partners with Anthropic to Scale Enterprise AI Across Global Clients
bullets: Tata Consultancy Services partnered with Anthropic to bring Claude AI to enterprise clients worldwide.
TCS gives Anthropic distribution through its global Fortune 500 customer base across industries.
The deal signals India's IT services sector is betting big on frontier AI integration.

articles.title: SpaceX (SPCX) Closes 19% Above IPO Price at $161 on Record-Breaking Nasdaq Debut
bullets: SpaceX stock opened at $150 and closed at $161.11, a 19% gain from its $135 IPO price.
At its session high of $168.75, SpaceX's market cap hit $2.21 trillion, near Amazon's $2.54T.
The IPO raised approximately $75 billion — the largest in market history.

articles.title: S&P 500 Gains 0.5%, Dow +0.7% on SpaceX IPO Boost and Iran Peace Deal Hopes
bullets: The S&P 500 gained 0.5%, the Nasdaq rose 0.3%, and the Dow advanced 0.7% on June 12.
SpaceX's strong debut lifted broader market sentiment alongside Iran peace deal progress reports.
Stocks were on track for a positive week as geopolitical risk appetite improved.

articles.title: Oil Prices Volatile as Iran Closes Strait of Hormuz, Peace Talks Emerge
bullets: Crude oil prices swung sharply as Iran declared Strait of Hormuz closed to shipping.
Prices pulled back from highs after reports of an imminent US-Iran deal framework emerged.
Energy analysts warn any deal collapse could push Brent crude to multi-year highs.

articles.title: Tech Sector Accounts for Nearly Half of All 2026 S&P 500 Earnings Growth
bullets: Technology companies are responsible for nearly half of total expected 2026 S&P 500 earnings growth.
AI-driven spending on chips, cloud, and software is the primary driver of tech sector outperformance.
Analysts note that concentration risk in tech remains one of the market's key vulnerabilities.

articles.title: Markets Eye Iran Deal and World Cup Sentiment as Trading Week Wraps Positive
bullets: Equity markets closed the week broadly higher, driven by peace deal optimism and the SpaceX IPO.
Investors are watching Iran negotiations closely as any deal would ease oil and shipping concerns.
Consumer sentiment may get a lift from US World Cup performance boosting retail and media spending.

articles.title: SpaceX IPO Makes Elon Musk World's First Likely Trillionaire; Company Valued at $2.1T
bullets: SpaceX's $2.1 trillion market cap after its IPO debut pushed Musk's net worth toward $1 trillion.
The company raised $75 billion in the largest IPO ever, dwarfing previous records.
Steve Eisman of 'The Big Short' is avoiding the stock, citing growing capital needs and concerns.

articles.title: KKR Launches $10B Helix Digital AI Infrastructure Company with Nvidia and Vistra
bullets: KKR launched Helix Digital Infrastructure with Nvidia, Vistra, and Kuwait Investment Authority backing.
Former AWS CEO Adam Selipsky will lead the $10 billion AI data center and power infrastructure firm.
The company will build data centers, power generation, and connectivity for AI hyperscalers.

articles.title: Amazon Borrows $17.5B from Banks to Fund Surging AI Infrastructure Spending
bullets: Amazon signed a deal to borrow $17.5 billion from banks for AI cloud and data center expansion.
The debt raise signals the scale of investment needed to compete with Microsoft, Google, and Oracle.
AI demand is reshaping Big Tech economics, turning infrastructure spending into a financing race.

articles.title: OpenAI Considers Deep Price Cuts as Competition with Anthropic Intensifies
bullets: OpenAI is weighing significant price reductions for its AI services to fend off Anthropic competition.
The move would shift the AI race from model benchmarks to price, distribution, and retention.
Lower AI pricing could accelerate enterprise adoption but squeeze margins across the sector.

articles.title: UK Consumer Confidence Falls Fastest Since 2022 as Middle East Conflict Bites
bullets: UK consumer confidence fell at its fastest quarterly rate since June 2022 amid Middle East tensions.
British households are bracing for renewed financial pressure as conflict dampens economic outlook.
Higher energy prices linked to the Strait of Hormuz closure are the primary concern for UK consumers.

articles.title: 77% of Americans Fear AI Data Centers Will Raise Electricity Bills, Poll Finds
bullets: Reuters/Ipsos poll: only 33% of Americans approve of rapid AI data center construction nationwide.
77% worry data centers will drive up electricity costs; just 14% would welcome one near their community.
14 states have considered moratoriums as local opposition delays projects across the country.

articles.title: EU Orders Meta to Open WhatsApp Business API to Rival AI Chatbots Within 5 Days
bullets: The European Commission issued interim measures requiring Meta to restore WhatsApp API access for AI rivals.
OpenAI and Anthropic assistants must be allowed access under the order; Meta plans to appeal.
The ruling aims to prevent dominant platforms from locking users into proprietary AI ecosystems.

articles.title: KKR and Nvidia Launch $10B Helix AI Infrastructure Company; Adam Selipsky CEO
bullets: KKR's Helix Digital Infrastructure will build the integrated backbone AI hyperscalers now need.
Former AWS CEO Adam Selipsky leads the venture backed by Nvidia, Vistra, and Kuwait sovereign wealth.
The $10B company will focus on AI data centers, power, and connectivity infrastructure at scale.

articles.title: Canada Proposes Bill to Ban Social Media for Under-16s and Regulate AI Chatbots
bullets: Canada introduced legislation that would ban social media for children under 16.
The bill also targets AI chatbot regulation, adding Canada to growing list of countries acting on youth safety.
Age verification, parental consent, and AI chatbot safeguards are all contested elements of the proposal.

articles.title: OpenAI: Chinese Influence Operations Used ChatGPT to Spread Content on US Tariffs
bullets: OpenAI uncovered covert Chinese influence operations using ChatGPT for US social media content.
Campaigns pushed claims about AI data centers raising electricity prices and framed tariffs geopolitically.
The report highlights generative AI's growing role as a tool in geopolitical influence operations.
